ai_enrichment.summary: A Base64-encoded PowerShell command was executed with explicit security bypasses (-ExecutionPolicy Bypass, -NoProfile, -NonInteractive), launched via cmd.exe. This pattern is a hallmark of malicious script execution commonly used by threat actors and malware frameworks (Cobalt Strike, Metasploit, etc.) to evade logging and execute arbitrary code. The encoded payload beginning with 'JABjAD0ATgBlAHcALQBPAGIAagBlAGMAdA==' decodes to '$c=New-Object', indicating object instantiation likely for download cradle or C2 communication.
investigation_steps: 1. Decode the full Base64 payload to reveal the complete PowerShell script and identify C2 URLs, download targets, or malicious functions
2. Query Sysmon Event ID 3 (Network Connection) for powershell.exe to identify any outbound C2 or download activity
3. Trace the parent cmd.exe process backward to identify the initial execution vector (what launched cmd.exe)
4. Search for Sysmon Event ID 7 (Image Loaded) to identify any suspicious DLLs loaded by this PowerShell process
5. Check for Sysmon Event ID 11 (File Created) correlated with this process to identify dropped payloads
6. Review labuser's recent activity: RDP logins, email attachments opened, browser downloads in the Desktop folder
7. Hunt across all endpoints for the same SHA256 hash and encoded command pattern
containment_actions: Immediately isolate Win10-Victim (192.168.56.102) from the network
Terminate the PowerShell process and any child processes
Disable the labuser account pending investigation
Block the decoded C2/download URLs at perimeter firewall and proxy
Capture memory dump of the endpoint before remediation for forensic analysis
Preserve full Sysmon and Windows Event logs from the host
ai_enrichment.false_positive_reasoning: Legitimate administrative scripts rarely combine ExecutionPolicy Bypass with EncodedCommand and NonInteractive flags simultaneously; this trifecta specifically evades security controls and is characteristic of offensive tooling rather than IT automation.
